official.name: Ted Cruz
official.office_address: 167 Russell Senate Office Building Washington DC 20510

summary.narrative: This term Cruz delivered on his signature No Tax on Tips pledge, which passed the Senate 100-0 and was enacted through the Working Families Tax Cut Act, and on tech-platform legislation via the enacted TAKE IT DOWN Act. As Commerce Committee chair he advanced aviation safety (ROTOR Act passed the Senate but was struck down in the House), innovation/spectrum measures, and small-business procurement reform, several of which cleared committee or one chamber but stalled short of enactment. Several Texas-specific commitments — water treaty enforcement, disaster relief, and the Sagebrush Lizard reversal — saw introduced legislation and advocacy but depend on external actors (Mexico, FEMA, the House) and remain incomplete. His transgender-sports ban remains advocacy and committee-stage legislation rather than an enacted prohibition.
summary.grade_rationale: The record shows full delivery on a clearly specified, high-clarity quantitative-adjacent promise (No Tax on Tips) plus a second enacted measure, with a cluster of qualitative committee-driven promises reaching the advanced (D3) milestone but stalling before enactment due to House or external dependencies. Promise quality is generally specific (clarity 3-5) with mostly significant (M2) magnitude, and Cruz's causal role is high (0.8) where he sponsored and drove legislation, lower (0.6) where outcomes depend on foreign governments, FEMA, or the NCAA.

data_gaps: Many delivery claims for Texas-specific items (Sagebrush Lizard, procurement) rest primarily on Cruz's own press releases [c204][c210] with limited independent corroboration — flagged LIMITED EVIDENCE. Several campaign 'promises' in the source (Keep Texas, Texas; safeguard freedoms; secure the border; school choice statements) were excluded as slogans or value statements lacking a verifiable forward-looking condition. The transgender-sports promise is classified Negative/preventative and is CONTESTED. Verification evidence is API-anchored and Tier-1 heavy, but some dated 2026 items fall within term and were retained.

provisional_status.display: Grade is record-to-date — term in progress, 24.4% elapsed (as of 2026-06-22). It will change as the term continues.
provisional_status.note: Distinct from any per-promise 'PROVISIONAL (early-term)' flag in scorecard.card_flags, which concerns a single promise's own deadline, not the term's completeness.
